pages.text: $(italic)Create$() is the pack's automation backbone — it turns $(bold)rotation$() into work. A spinning source drives shafts and cogwheels, and those drive machines that crush, press, mix, and move things for you.$(br2)This is only a first taste. The full system, and every add-on, has its own book: the $(italic)Create Compendium$() (see $(l:7_catalog/create_compendium)The Library$()).
pages.title: Create, in Brief

pages.text: $(bold)Let Ponder teach you.$() Create ships an animated in-game guide called $(italic)Ponder$(). Open your inventory or the recipe viewer, hover any Create item, and press $(bold)W$() to watch a short scene that shows exactly how it works.$(br2)Ponder is the fastest way to learn any one block — this guide won't repeat what it already shows so well.
pages.title: Ponder

pages.text: $(bold)Getting started.$()$(br)$(li)$(bold)Andesite Alloy$() is the gateway material — nearly every early machine needs it. Make a stack first.$(li)Your first rotation can come from a $(bold)Hand Crank$() or a $(bold)Water Wheel$(); a $(bold)Windmill$() of sails comes soon after.$(li)A $(bold)Millstone$() grinds ores and plants and can even be hand-cranked before you have real power.
pages.title: First Steps

pages.text: $(bold)Two tools to keep on you.$()$(br)$(li)$(bold)Engineer's Goggles$() — wear them to see a machine's $(italic)speed$() and $(italic)stress$() at a glance. Every machine costs stress; every source supplies it.$(li)$(bold)Wrench$() — rotates and dismantles Create blocks cleanly.$(br2)When something stalls, put the goggles on first — it's almost always stress or speed.
pages.title: Goggles & Wrench
